mission.title: traefik: Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI
mission.description: Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI. Requested by 48+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
I'd love to see SSL/TLS certificate status in the web UI. For example:
- see each domain's certificate
- status of each certificate, date of last renewal, expiry
- any to messages pertinent to the certificate's renewal.

Thanks for all the work!

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI
steps.description:
At least partially

### What does this PR do?

This PR creates a dedicated view for certificate information. I locally already have more versions, which would allow renewal or revocation / deletion of ACME certificates, but the changes are too many and too wild. So I thought, maybe first start with showing some information.

It was more difficult than I thought. Reason one is that I am not so familiar with go and vite. The second "issue" I had is that a certificate does not have a key to

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade traefik to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade traefik:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade traefik traefik/traefik --namespace traefik
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik
helm list -n traefik
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature request : SSL/TLS certificate status in web UI" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
At least partially

### What does this PR do?

This PR creates a dedicated view for certificate information. I locally already have more versions, which would allow renewal or revocation / deletion of ACME certificates, but the changes are too many and too wild. So I thought, maybe first start with showing some information.

It was more difficult than I thought.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
